annotations.label: Data structure (organizing data)

anchor.snippet: a data structure is a way to organize and store data

annotations.note: Mathlib is a mathematics library and does not formalize the general informal CS notion of a data structure.

annotations.label: Data structure as physical implementation of a data type

anchor.snippet: a data structure is the physical implementation of a data type

annotations.note: This is an informal implementation-level definition with no corresponding declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rob Pike: data structure impact on efficiency

anchor.snippet: the choice of data structure almost always has a greater impact on efficiency than the choice of algorithm

annotations.note: An informal programming aphorism, not a mathematical statement present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Data structure corresponds to a single concrete implementation

anchor.snippet: A data structure corresponds directly to a single concrete implementation

annotations.note: A claim about software implementation with no formal counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiple data structures for the same ADT

anchor.snippet: There may be multiple concrete data structures for the same ADT

annotations.note: A statement about abstract data types versus implementations, outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Array

anchor.snippet: An array is a number of elements in a specific order

annotations.note: The `Array` type is defined in Lean core/Batteries, not in `Mathlib/`, where no `Array` definition exists.

annotations.label: Linked list

anchor.snippet: is a linear collection of data elements of any type, called nodes

annotations.note: Lists are provided by Lean core's `List`; Mathlib only adds lemmas and does not define the linked-list structure itself.

annotations.label: Record
annotations.note: Records are a Lean language feature (`structure`), not a formalized object in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Hash table

anchor.snippet: are data structures that provide fast retrieval of values based on keys

annotations.note: No `HashMap`/hash-table definition is present in `Mathlib/` (it lives in Lean core/Batteries).

annotations.label: Graph

anchor.snippet: Graphs are collections of nodes connected by edges

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph` formalizes graphs as a symmetric irreflexive adjacency relation, but as a mathematical (simple, undirected) object rather than the general directed/multigraph data structure.

annotations.label: Stacks and queues

anchor.snippet: Stacks and queues are abstract data types that can be implemented

annotations.note: No stack or queue data-structure definition exists in `Mathlib/` (only incidental string matches like `StackTuringMachine`).

annotations.label: Tree

anchor.snippet: Trees represent a hierarchical organization of elements

annotations.note: `Tree` is an inductive binary tree with values at nodes, a specific instance of the general hierarchical tree data structure.

annotations.label: Trie

anchor.snippet: A trie , or prefix tree, is a special type of tree

annotations.note: No trie/prefix-tree definition exists in `Mathlib/`; the discrimination-tree (`DiscrTree`) metaprogramming utility is unrelated infrastructure, not a formalized trie.
